carry_forward.detail: all_gpus.cpp is CPU-side host code (GPU enumeration); no device kernels modified; device bitcode on gfx90a/gfx1100 is unchanged (cooperativeLaunch=1 on those platforms, condition was already satisfied

all_gpus.cpp is CPU-side host code (GPU enumeration); no device kernels modified; device bitcode on gfx90a/gfx1100 is unchanged (cooperativeLaunch=1 on those platforms, condition was already satisfied

windows-gfx1101.blocked_reason: Same Windows cooperative-launch gap as gfx1201: hipLaunchCooperativeKernel returns 719 (cooperativeLaunch=0) on gfx1101 too under ROCm 7.14, proven directly on this host (1x1 plain+grid.sync probe, _rocm_sdk_core 7.14 runtime). MSM cannot validate; NTT 7/7 and bn254 gate would pass (RDNA3, as on Linux gfx1100) but the platform cannot reach completed. Optional arch; recorded blocked so it is not re-selected to reproduce the known MSM failure.

windows-gfx1201.blocked_reason: MSM (Pippenger) requires hipLaunchCooperativeKernel, which is unsupported on Windows ROCm 7.14: cooperativeLaunch=0 and the launch API returns hipErrorLaunchFailure(719) for ANY kernel (even a 1x1 plain kernel), confirmed on this host across all 3 available runtimes (System32 Adrenalin amdhip64, TheRock _rocm_sdk_core 7.14, TheRock build/bin 7.0.2) and on BOTH GPUs (gfx1201 RDNA4 and gfx1101 RDNA3). Not a wrong-runtime artifact and not RDNA4-specific; the same RDNA3 silicon supports cooperative launch on Linux (gfx1100 ROCm 7.2.1 passes MSM). NTT 7/7 fields and the bn254 build-gate pass on Windows. Port code is correct; this is a Windows HIP runtime capability gap. The Windows tier cannot be satisfied for MSM on this host (gfx1101 has the same gap).

windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: windows-gfx1151 host retired (superseded by the gfx1101/gfx1201 machine); validation moved to windows-gfx1101 and windows-gfx1201
